0.title: identification of small-molecule activators for gpr151 receptor in human cell-based assay

categories.reason: The assay is aimed at identifying activators of GPR151, a gene that is predominantly expressed in the central nervous system. Therefore, it could be deduced that the assay is related to the study of potential neurotoxic effects initiated through this pathway.

metadata.BioAssay Name: Cell-based high throughput primary assay to identify activators of GPR151
metadata.Source Name: The Scripps Research Institute Molecular Screening Center

1.title: high-throughput biochemical assay for mitf inhibition detection

categories.reason: The assay is primarily for the purpose of identifying inhibitors of the microphthalmia-associated transcription factor (MITF). This assay does not directly align with any toxicological endpoints.

metadata.BioAssay Name: AlphaScreen-based biochemical high throughput primary assay to identify inhibitors of microphthalmia-associated transcription factor (MITF)
metadata.Source Name: The Scripps Research Institute Molecular Screening Center

2.title: identification of e3 ligase activators via alphascreen biochemical assay

categories.category: kinetics (pharmacokinetics, toxicokinetics, adme, cmax, auc, etc)
categories.reason: The assay is identified as a biochemical high throughput primary assay designed to identify activators of the E3 ligase (FBW7). This suggests that substances are being screened for their ability to interact with this enzyme, which is key to many biological processes including protein degradation, cell cycle regulation, and signal transduction. These are fundamental aspects of toxicokinetics and pharmacokinetics, which involve the study of how substances are absorbed, distributed, metabolized, and excreted in the body.

metadata.BioAssay Name: AlphaScreen-based biochemical high throughput primary assay to identify activators of the E3 ligase (FBW7).
metadata.Source Name: The Scripps Research Institute Molecular Screening Center

3.title: inhibition assay of tead-yap interaction using luminescent cell-based screening

categories.reason: The assay is evaluating the ability of small-molecule substances to inhibit the TEAD-YAP protein interaction, which is known to play a key role in cell growth and tissue development but does not directly link to any specific type of toxicity. Therefore, it does not fit neatly into any of the given categories.

metadata.BioAssay Name: Luminescence cell-based high throughput primary assay to identify inhibitors of TEAD-YAP interaction.
metadata.Source Name: The Scripps Research Institute Molecular Screening Center

4.title: evaluation of small-molecule inhibitors of human 12s-lipoxygenase (alox12) from ncats

categories.reason: Arachidonate 12S-lipoxygenase (ALOX12) is involved in the metabolism of arachidonic acid, a key process in cell signaling. Hence, substances tested in this assay may have impacts on metabolic processes.

The assay is for inhibitors of human arachidonate 12S-lipoxygenase (ALOX12), an enzyme involved in the metabolism of arachidonic acid, a key factor in neuronal inflammation and neurodegenerative diseases. Hence, the assay could indirectly indicate a substance's potential for neurotoxicity.

metadata.BioAssay Name: Primary qHTS assay for inhibitors of human arachidonate 12S-lipoxygenase (ALOX12): Round 2
metadata.Source Name: National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS)

5.title: assay for ldha inhibition in human enzyme targets using small molecules

categories.reason: The assay is testing for inhibitors of human lactate dehydrogenase, a crucial enzyme involved in metabolism, specifically the conversion of lactate to pyruvate. Therefore, chemicals that inhibit this enzyme could potentially cause metabolic toxicity.

metadata.BioAssay Name: qHTS assay for inhibitors of human lactate dehydrogenase
metadata.Project Category: Molecular Libraries Probe Production Centers Network
metadata.Source Name: National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS)

6.title: high-throughput alphascreen assay for identifying small-molecule inhibitors of ssb-pria in antibiotic resistance

categories.reason: The assay is being used to measure the interaction between a small molecule and the SSB-PriA antibiotic resistant target, which is a physical interaction.

metadata.Source Name: Small Molecule Screening Facility, UW Madison

1872.title: discovery of small-molecule inhibitors targeting zika virus ns2b-ns3 protease via gu rhodamine qhts

categories.reason: The assay is for identifying inhibitors of the Zika virus, and does not relate directly to any of the predefined toxicity categories.

metadata.BioAssay Name: Primary screen GU Rhodamine qHTS for Zika virus inhibitors: Unlinked NS2B-NS3 protease assay
metadata.Source Name: National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS)
